turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#a8e0ff"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#d8f3ff"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="sea" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#0077be"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#004a7a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sunGrad" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff9cc"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffe68a"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffb347"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- sky -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="260" fill="url(#sky)" />

  <!-- sun on horizon -->
  <circle cx="380" cy="200" r="60" fill="url(#sunGrad)" opacity="0.95" />

  <!-- distant lighter water band (horizon glow) -->
  <rect x="0" y="240" width="512" height="16" fill="#66b7d9" opacity="0.9" />

  <!-- sea base -->
  <rect x="0" y="256" width="512" height="256" fill="url(#sea)" />

  <!-- layered waves near horizon -->
  <path d="M0 276 C48 260 96 292 144 276 C192 260 240 292 288 276 C336 260 384 292 432 276 C480 260 528 292 576 276 L576 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#036ca8" opacity="0.9" transform="translate(-64,0)" />
  <path d="M0 300 C40 284 80 316 120 300 C160 284 200 316 240 300 C280 284 320 316 360 300 C400 284 440 316 480 300 C520 284 560 316 600 300 L600 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#0280b8" opacity="0.85" transform="translate(-88,0)" />

  <!-- white foam crests -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M30 292 C50 282 70 302 90 292 C110 282 130 302 150 292 C170 282 190 302 210 292 L210 304 C190 314 170 294 150 304 C130 314 110 294 90 304 C70 314 50 294 30 304 Z" opacity="0.8" transform="translate(0,0)"/>
    <path d="M260 320 C284 308 308 328 332 320 C356 312 380 332 404 320 L404 332 C380 344 356 324 332 332 C308 340 284 320 260 332 Z" opacity="0.7"/>
    <path d="M420 288 C436 278 452 296 468 288 C484 280 500 298 516 288 L516 300 C500 308 484 290 468 300 C452 310 436 292 420 300 Z" opacity="0.75"/>
  </g>

  <!-- small ripple highlights -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.6">
    <path d="M60 360 C100 348 140 372 180 360" />
    <path d="M120 400 C160 388 200 412 240 400" />
    <path d="M300 440 C340 428 380 452 420 440" />
  </g>

  <!-- simple birds (stylized) -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#333" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M120 120 C132 108 148 108 160 120" />
    <path d="M160 120 C172 108 188 108 200 120" />
    <path d="M220 100 C232 88 248 88 260 100" />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle darker band at bottom for depth -->
  <rect x="0" y="440" width="512" height="72" fill="#033b58" opacity="0.35" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">

      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#d8f3ff"/>

      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#004a7a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sunGrad" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff9cc"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#ffe68a"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ffb347"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- sky -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="260" fill="url(#sky)" />

  <!-- sun on horizon -->
  <circle cx="380" cy="200" r="60" fill="url(#sunGrad)" opacity="0.95" />

  <!-- distant lighter water band (horizon glow) -->
  <rect x="0" y="240" width="512" height="16" fill="#66b7d9" opacity="0.9" />

  <!-- sea base -->
  <rect x="0" y="256" width="512" height="256" fill="url(#sea)" />

  <!-- layered waves near horizon -->
  <path d="M0 276 C48 260 96 292 144 276 C192 260 240 292 288 276 C336 260 384 292 432 276 C480 260 528 292 576 276 L576 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#036ca8" opacity="0.9" transform="translate(-64,0)" />
  <path d="M0 300 C40 284 80 316 120 300 C160 284 200 316 240 300 C280 284 320 316 360 300 C400 284 440 316 480 300 C520 284 560 316 600 300 L600 512 L0 512 Z" fill="#0280b8" opacity="0.85" transform="translate(-88,0)" />

  <!-- white foam crests -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M30 292 C50 282 70 302 90 292 C110 282 130 302 150 292 C170 282 190 302 210 292 L210 304 C190 314 170 294 150 304 C130 314 110 294 90 304 C70 314 50 294 30 304 Z" opacity="0.8" transform="translate(0,0)"/>
    <path d="M260 320 C284 308 308 328 332 320 C356 312 380 332 404 320 L404 332 C380 344 356 324 332 332 C308 340 284 320 260 332 Z" opacity="0.7"/>
    <path d="M420 288 C436 278 452 296 468 288 C484 280 500 298 516 288 L516 300 C500 308 484 290 468 300 C452 310 436 292 420 300 Z" opacity="0.75"/>
  </g>

  <!-- small ripple highlights -->
  <g fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.6">
    <path d="M60 360 C100 348 140 372 180 360" />

  <!-- simple birds (stylized) -->

  <!-- subtle darker band at bottom for depth -->
  <rect x="0" y="440" width="512" height="72" fill="#033b58" opacity="0.35" />
</svg>"

message.reasoning:
**Designing SVG Content**

I'm thinking about using a radial gradient for the sun and placing the sun circle at (400,140) with a radius of 60, partially overlapping the horizon. For the horizon, I’ll create a rectangle at y=200 with a height of 312.

Then, for the sea waves, I want to draw cubic bezier paths for repeating waves with a fill similar to the sea but slightly darker. I’ll add layered paths for wave crests in white, and consider using dark gray strokes for the seagulls. Let's keep the SVG simple!

**Designing SVG Content**

I'm thinking about using a radial gradient for the sun and placing the sun circle at (400,140) with a radius of 60, partially overlapping the horizon. For the horizon, I’ll create a rectangle at y=200 with a height of 312.

Then, for the sea waves, I want to draw cubic bezier paths for repeating waves with a fill similar to the sea but slightly darker. I’ll add layered paths for wave crests in white, and consider using dark gray strokes for the seagulls. Let's keep the SVG simple!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
